root_text.sn46.30:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sumbhesu viharati setakaṁ nāma sumbhānaṁ nigamo.
root_text.sn46.30:1.2: Atha kho āyasmā udāyī yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami …pe… ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā udāyī bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn46.30:2.2: Yāva bahukatañca me, bhante, bhagavati pemañca gāravo ca hirī ca ottappañca.
root_text.sn46.30:2.3: Ahañhi, bhante, pubbe agārikabhūto samāno abahukato ahosiṁ dhammena abahukato saṅghena.
root_text.sn46.30:2.4: So khvāhaṁ bhagavati pemañca gāravañca hiriñca ottappañca sampassamāno agārasmā anagāriyaṁ pabbajito.
root_text.sn46.30:2.6: ‘iti rūpaṁ, iti rūpassa samudayo, iti rūpassa atthaṅgamo;
root_text.sn46.30:2.10: iti viññāṇaṁ, iti viññāṇassa samudayo, iti viññāṇassa atthaṅgamo’ti.
root_text.sn46.30:3.1: So khvāhaṁ, bhante, suññāgāragato imesaṁ pañcupādānakkhandhānaṁ ukkujjāvakujjaṁ samparivattento ‘idaṁ dukkhan’ti yathābhūtaṁ abbhaññāsiṁ, ‘ayaṁ dukkhasamudayo’ti yathābhūtaṁ abbhaññāsiṁ, ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodho’ti yathābhūtaṁ abbhaññāsiṁ, ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti yathābhūtaṁ abbhaññāsiṁ.
root_text.sn46.30:3.2: Dhammo ca me, bhante, abhisamito, maggo ca me paṭiladdho;
root_text.sn46.30:3.3: yo me bhāvito bahulīkato tathā tathā viharantaṁ tathattāya upanessati yathāhaṁ:
root_text.sn46.30:3.4: ‘khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyā’ti pajānissāmi.
root_text.sn46.30:4.1: Satisambojjhaṅgo me, bhante, paṭiladdho, yo me bhāvito bahulīkato tathā tathā viharantaṁ tathattāya upanessati yathāhaṁ:
root_text.sn46.30:4.2: ‘khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyā’ti pajānissāmi …pe…
root_text.sn46.30:4.3: upekkhāsambojjhaṅgo me, bhante, paṭiladdho, yo me bhāvito bahulīkato tathā tathā viharantaṁ tathattāya upanessati yathāhaṁ:
root_text.sn46.30:4.4: ‘khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyā’ti pajānissāmi.
root_text.sn46.30:4.5: Ayaṁ kho me, bhante, maggo paṭiladdho, yo me bhāvito bahulīkato tathā tathā viharantaṁ tathattāya upanessati yathāhaṁ:
root_text.sn46.30:4.6: ‘khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyā’ti pajānissāmī”ti.
root_text.sn46.30:5.2: Eso hi te, udāyi, maggo paṭiladdho, yo te bhāvito bahulīkato tathā tathā viharantaṁ tathattāya upanessati yathā tvaṁ:
root_text.sn46.30:5.3: ‘khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyā’ti pajānissasī”ti.

translation_text.sn46.30:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying in the land of the Sumbhas, near the town of the Sumbhas named Sedaka.
translation_text.sn46.30:1.2: Then Venerable Udāyī went up to the Buddha … and said to him:
translation_text.sn46.30:2.2: How helpful my love and respect for the Buddha have been, and my sense of conscience and prudence.
translation_text.sn46.30:2.3: For when I was still a layman, I wasn’t helped much by the teaching or the Saṅgha.
translation_text.sn46.30:2.4: But when I considered my love and respect for the Buddha, and my sense of conscience and prudence, I went forth from the lay life to homelessness.
translation_text.sn46.30:2.5: The Buddha taught me the Dhamma:
translation_text.sn46.30:2.6: ‘Such is form, such is the origin of form, such is the disappearance of form.
translation_text.sn46.30:2.10: Such is consciousness, such is the origin of consciousness, such is the disappearance of consciousness.’
translation_text.sn46.30:3.1: Then, while staying in an empty hut, I followed the churning of the five grasping aggregates. I truly understood: ‘This is suffering’ … ‘This is the origin of suffering’ … ‘This is the cessation of suffering’ … ‘This is the practice that leads to the cessation of suffering’.
translation_text.sn46.30:3.2: I comprehended the teaching; I acquired the path.
translation_text.sn46.30:3.3: When developed and cultivated as I’m living in such a way, it will bring me to such a state that I will understand:
translation_text.sn46.30:3.4: ‘Rebirth is ended, the spiritual journey has been completed, what had to be done has been done, there is nothing further for this place.’
translation_text.sn46.30:4.1: I acquired the awakening factors of mindfulness, investigation of principles, energy, rapture, tranquility, immersion, and equanimity.
translation_text.sn46.30:4.3: When developed and cultivated as I’m living in such a way, they will bring me to such a state that I will understand:
translation_text.sn46.30:4.4: ‘Rebirth is ended, the spiritual journey has been completed, what had to be done has been done, there is nothing further for this place.’
translation_text.sn46.30:4.5: This is the path that I acquired. When developed and cultivated as I’m living in such a way, it will bring me to such a state that I will understand:
translation_text.sn46.30:4.6: ‘Rebirth is ended, the spiritual journey has been completed, what had to be done has been done, there is nothing further for this place.’”
translation_text.sn46.30:5.2: For that is indeed the path that you acquired. When developed and cultivated as you’re living in such a way, it will bring you to such a state that you will understand:
translation_text.sn46.30:5.3: ‘Rebirth is ended, the spiritual journey has been completed, what had to be done has been done, there is nothing further for this place.’”
